behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output content (signature it generates), not just wrapper logic. A stubbed model could always return the expected signature and hide real mismatch failures. Not Workflow because this behavior is a single model-decision quality check, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real generation from NL specs with strict interface requirements, parse generated code signature, and assert exact parameter names/types/return type match expected interface.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration (attempt loop + test executor + bookkeeping). This does not require real OS/network/subprocess, and does not depend on model output quality if model/test results are deterministically stubbed. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is about control flow ordering/early exit, not LLM decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic fake generator and fake test runner (fail then pass), assert attempt order, assert stop after first pass, and assert no extra generation/test call is made.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real agentic steps in sequence (generate -> test feedback -> refine -> retest) and success depends on LLM behavior across attempts. A mock would hide whether iterative refinement actually recovers from initial failure.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known prompt where first live attempt commonly fails; run real loop with max-attempt policy and real test execution; assert at least one subsequent attempt passes within allowed attempts.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily cross-module data handoff (test result objects -> prompt builder -> request payload). Real LLM output quality is not required to verify inclusion, and no real OS/network/subprocess is required. Rejected Agentic because behavior text checks prompt content inclusion, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run failing test cycle with deterministic stubs, capture the next prompt payload sent to model client, and assert failed test names/messages are present and correctly mapped.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: Needs sequential agentic dependence: prior failure context must influence next generation step. This is more than single-call quality; it verifies cross-attempt adaptation by the model. Stubbed outputs can trivially differ or match and would not validate real adaptive behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to produce failing code, feed real failure context into attempt 2, then assert attempt-2 code meaningfully differs from attempt-1 in failure-relevant regions (not just whitespace/comments).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-bookkeeping and error-shape verification across modules. Deterministic failing stubs can reproduce the failure boundary. No real infrastructure or model-quality dependence is required. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is about stop condition and aggregation, not live model correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use deterministic always-fail generator/test-runner stubs, execute loop, assert exactly 3 attempts executed, assert structured error schema includes all 3 attempt artifacts and test outcomes.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to non-agentic deterministic wiring. Minimum boundary is end-to-end local module integration. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess is required by the behavior text; rejected Agentic/Workflow because model output quality is intentionally stubbed out.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end service flow in-process with deterministic model stub and test-runner fixture, verifying generation invocation, test execution sequencing, and attempt bookkeeping outputs.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full live multi-step agent loop with sequential model decisions and refinement. Success criterion is model-driven solution quality over iterations; stubs would hide real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run real model + real loop on a curated hard prompt where attempt 1 is expected to fail, then assert eventual pass within allowed retries and record per-attempt outcomes.
